title: Exhaustive if statement generates latches
body:
### Version

0.24

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

The following code should not generate latches:
```sv
module mux (
  input wire select_1,
  input wire select_0,
  input wire in_0,
  input wire in_1,
  input wire in_2,
  input wire in_3,
  output wire out
);

always @(*) begin
  if (!select_1 && !select_0) begin
    out = in_0;
  end else if (!select_1 && select_0) begin
    out = in_1;
  end else if (select_1 && !select_0) begin
    out = in_2;
  end else if (select_1 && select_0) begin
    out = in_3;
  end
end

endmodule
```

### Expected Behavior

A all of the cases are covered by the if statement, this should synthesize without a latch.

### Actual Behavior

A latch is generated.
![yosys_show](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/assets/64753595/382fc2c7-9676-48ea-803d-4a2a69c930af)
code_blocks:
module mux (
  input wire select_1,
  input wire select_0,
  input wire in_0,
  input wire in_1,
  input wire in_2,
  input wire in_3,
  output wire out
);

always @(*) begin
  if (!select_1 && !select_0) begin
    out = in_0;
  end else if (!select_1 && select_0) begin
    out = in_1;
  end else if (select_1 && !select_0) begin
    out = in_2;
  end else if (select_1 && select_0) begin
    out = in_3;
  end
end

endmodule
